name: Mega Man
description:
Mega Man is a side-scrolling action platformer where the player controls the titular character, a humanoid robot. The game is structured as a series of discrete levels, each culminating in a boss battle against a unique Robot Master. Mega Man can run, jump, and fire a projectile weapon called the Mega Buster. 

Before starting the levels, there is a level select screen. The player chooses which Robot Master level to tackle. Each level is filled with various enemy robots, environmental hazards, and platforming challenges. Upon defeating a Robot Master, Mega Man absorbs their primary weapon, gaining a new ability that can be used throughout the game and is particularly effective against certain other Robot Masters. These new weapons often have limited ammo. Mega Man can switch between acquired weapons at any time. The player must navigate these levels, overcoming obstacles and defeating enemies to reach the final boss and complete the game. Dying in a level sends the player back to the start of the level or checkpoint.

Mega Man has a health bar. Taking damage from enemies and hazards depletes the health bar. If the health bar reaches zero, the player loses a life, resetting to the level start or a checkpoint if one was reached. Game over is triggered when all lives are lost. The game features power-ups hidden throughout the levels which can restore health, ammo, or grant temporary invincibility.

controls.z: Fire Mega Buster or equipped weapon
controls.arrow_down: Slide (if available after acquiring a specific weapon/ability)
